session.tool_result:
1→"""Abstract interface for remote GitHub operations without a local git clone. 2→ 3→This gateway enables GitHub operations (branch creation, file commits, PR management, 4→workflow dispatch) using the GitHub REST API directly, without requiring a local 5→git repository. All methods take explicit owner/repo parameters. 6→""" 7→ 8→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 9→ 10→ 11→class RemoteGitHub(ABC): 12→ """GitHub operations that don't require a local git clone. 13→ 14→ Every method takes explicit owner/repo parameters instead of repo_root: Path. 15→ Uses the GitHub REST API via HttpClient. 16→ """ 17→ 18→ @abstractmethod 19→ def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str: 20→ """Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user. 21→ 22→ Returns: 23→ GitHub username string 24→ """ 25→ ... 26→ 27→ @abstractmethod 28→ def get_default_branch_name(self, *, owner: str, repo: str) -> str: 29→ """Get the default branch name for a repository. 30→
... [174 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Real implementation of RemoteGitHub using HttpClient. 2→ 3→All GitHub operations are performed via the REST API through HttpClient, 4→with no local git repository or gh CLI required. 5→""" 6→ 7→import base64 8→import secrets 9→import string 10→ 11→from erk_shared.gateway.http.abc import HttpClient 12→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 13→from erk_shared.gateway.time.abc import Time 14→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 15→ 16→ 17→class RealRemoteGitHub(RemoteGitHub): 18→ """Production implementation using HttpClient for all GitHub API calls.""" 19→ 20→ def __init__(self, *, http_client: HttpClient, time: Time) -> None: 21→ """Create RealRemoteGitHub. 22→ 23→ Args: 24→ http_client: Authenticated HTTP client for GitHub API 25→ time: Time gateway for polling delays 26→ """ 27→ self._http = http_client 28→ self._time = time 29→ 30→ def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str:
... [201 lines omitted] ...
35→ raise RuntimeError("GitHub API returned user without login field") 43→ raise RuntimeError( 54→ raise RuntimeError( 88→ raise RuntimeError( 117→ raise RuntimeError("GitHub API returned PR without number field") 188→ raise RuntimeError( 199→ raise RuntimeError(

1→"""Fake implementation of RemoteGitHub for testing. 2→ 3→Constructor-injected responses for reads, mutation tracking lists for write assertions. 4→""" 5→ 6→from dataclasses import dataclass 7→ 8→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 9→ 10→ 11→@dataclass(frozen=True) 12→class CreatedRef: 13→ """Recorded create_ref call.""" 14→ 15→ owner: str 16→ repo: str 17→ ref: str 18→ sha: str 19→ 20→ 21→@dataclass(frozen=True) 22→class CreatedFileCommit: 23→ """Recorded create_file_commit call.""" 24→ 25→ owner: str 26→ repo: str 27→ path: str 28→ content: str 29→ message: str 30→ branch: str
... [249 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Data types for GitHub issues integration.""" 2→ 3→from collections.abc import Iterator 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→from datetime import datetime 6→ 7→from erk_shared.non_ideal_state import EnsurableResult 8→ 9→ 10→@dataclass(frozen=True) 11→class IssueInfo: 12→ """Information about a GitHub issue.""" 13→ 14→ number: int 15→ title: str 16→ body: str 17→ state: str # "OPEN" or "CLOSED" 18→ url: str 19→ labels: list[str] 20→ assignees: list[str] 21→ created_at: datetime 22→ updated_at: datetime 23→ author: str # GitHub login of the issue creator 24→ 25→ 26→@dataclass(frozen=True) 27→class IssueNotFound: 28→ """Sentinel indicating an issue was not found. 29→ 30→ Used as part of union return types for LBYL-style error handling.
... [71 lines omitted] ...
71→ in T | GitHubAPIFailed union return types.

1→"""Command to validate PR rules or plan format for the current branch.""" 2→ 3→from dataclasses import dataclass 4→from pathlib import Path 5→from typing import NamedTuple 6→ 7→import click 8→ 9→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 10→from erk.cli.github_parsing import parse_issue_identifier 11→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 12→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 13→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.abc import GitHubIssues 14→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound 15→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import find_metadata_block 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.plan_header import extract_plan_from_comment 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.schemas import PlanHeaderSchema 18→from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.types import BlockKeys 19→from erk_shared.gateway.github.pr_footer import ( 20→ extract_header_from_body, 21→ is_header_at_legacy_position, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 24→from erk_shared.gateway.pr.submit import has_checkout_footer_for_pr 25→from erk_shared.impl_folder import read_plan_ref, resolve_impl_dir 26→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 27→from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import ( 28→ extract_plan_content, 29→ has_original_plan_section, 30→)
... [314 lines omitted] ...
43→ """Validation completed (may have passed or failed checks). 48→ failed_count: Number of failed checks 53→ failed_count: int 57→class PlanValidationError: 58→ """Could not complete validation (API error, network issue, etc.).""" 60→ error: str 63→PlanValidationResult = PlanValidationSuccess | PlanValidationError 78→ It does not produce output or raise SystemExit. It never raises exceptions - 79→ API failures are returned as PlanValidationError. 87→ PlanValidationSuccess if validation completed (may have passed or failed checks) 88→ PlanValidationError if unable to complete validation (API error, etc.) 96→ return PlanValidationError(error=f"Plan #{plan_number} not found") 112→ except ValueError as e: 113→ # Extract first error message for cleaner output 114→ error_msg = str(e).split("\n")[0] 115→ checks.append((False, f"plan-header validation failed: {error_msg}")) 130→ except RuntimeError as e: 131→ return PlanValidationError(error=str(e)) 147→ failed_count = sum(1 for passed, _ in checks if not passed) 150→ passed=failed_count == 0, 152→ failed_count=failed_count, 202→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Not in a git repository") 213→ if isinstance(result, PlanValidationError): 214→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Failed to validate plan: {result.error}") 227→ check_word = "checks" if result.failed_count > 1 else "check" 230→ f"Plan validation failed ({result.failed_count} {check_word} failed)", fg="red" 251→ click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"No pull request found for branch '{branch}'" 331→ failed_count = sum(1 for c in checks if not c.passed) 332→ if failed_count == 0: 336→ check_word = "check" if failed_count == 1 else "checks" 337→ user_output(click.style(f"{failed_count} {check_word} failed", fg="red"))

session.tool_result: Launching skill: dignified-python
session.user:
Base directory for this skill: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/dignified-python
# Dignified Python Coding Standards
## Core Knowledge (ALWAYS Loaded)
@dignified-python-core.md
## Version Detection
**Identify the project's minimum Python version** by checking (in order):
1. `pyproject.toml` - Look for `requires-python` field (e.g., `requires-python = ">=3.12"`) 2. `setup.py` or `setup.cfg` - Look for `python_requires` 3. `.python-version` file - Contains version like `3.12` or `3.12.0` 4. Default to Python 3.12 if no version specifier found
**Once identified, load the appropriate version-specific file:**
- Python 3.10: Load `versions/python-3.10.md` - Python 3.11: Load `versions/python-3.11.md` - Python 3.12: Load `versions/python-3.12.md` - Python 3.13: Load `versions/python-3.13.md`
## Conditional Loading (Load Based on Task Patterns)
Core files above cover 80%+ of Python code patterns. Only load these additional files when you detect specific patterns:
Pattern detection examples:
- If task mentions "click" or "CLI" -> Load `cli-patterns.md` - If task mentions "subprocess" -> Load `subprocess.md`
## When to Read Each Reference Document
The `references/` directory contains detailed guidance for specialized topics. Load these on-demand when you encounter relevant patterns:
### `references/exception-handling.md`
**Read when**:
- Writing try/except blocks - Wrapping third-party APIs that may raise - Seeing or writing `from e` or `from None` - Unsure if LBYL alternative exists
### `references/interfaces.md`
**Read when**:
- Creating ABC or Protocol classes - Writing @abstractmethod decorators - Designing gateway layer interfaces - Choosing between ABC and Protocol
### `references/typing-advanced.md`
**Read when**:
- Using typing.cast() - Creating Literal type aliases - Narrowing types in conditional blocks
### `references/module-design.md`
**Read when**:
- Creating new Python modules - Adding module-level code (beyond simple constants) - Using @cache decorator at module level - Seeing Path() or computation at module level - Considering inline imports
### `references/api-design.md`
**Read when**:
- Adding default parameter values to functions - Defining functions with 5 or more parameters - Using ThreadPoolExecutor.submit() - Reviewing function signatures
### `references/checklists.md`
**Read when**:
- Final review before committing Python code - Unsure if you've followed all rules - Need a quick lookup of requirements
## How to Use This Skill
1. **Core knowledge** is loaded automatically (LBYL, pathlib, basic imports, anti-patterns) 2. **Version detection** happens once - identify the minimum Python version and load the appropriate version file 3. **Reference documents** are loaded on-demand based on the triggers above 4. **Additional patterns** may require extra loading (CLI patterns, subprocess) 5. **Each file is self-contained** with complete guidance for its domain
session.assistant: Now let me read the core dignified-python rules and detect the Python version:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→--- 3→ 4→# Dignified Python - Core Standards 5→ 6→This document contains the core Python coding standards that apply to 80%+ of Python code. These principles are loaded with every skill invocation. 7→ 8→For conditional loading of specialized patterns: 9→ 10→- CLI development -> Load `cli-patterns.md` 11→- Subprocess operations -> Load `subprocess.md` 12→ 13→For detailed reference material, see the "When to Read Each Reference" section in SKILL.md. 14→ 15→--- 16→ 17→## The Cornerstone: LBYL Over EAFP 18→ 19→**Look Before You Leap: Check conditions proactively, NEVER use exceptions for control flow.** 20→ 21→This is the single most important rule in dignified Python. Every pattern below flows from this principle. 22→ 23→```python 24→# CORRECT: Check first 25→if key in mapping: 26→ value = mapping[key] 27→ process(value) 28→ 29→# WRONG: Exception as control flow 30→try:
... [502 lines omitted] ...
33→except KeyError: 39→## Exception Handling Basics 45→LBYL means checking conditions before acting. EAFP (Easier to Ask for Forgiveness than Permission) means trying operations and catching exceptions. In dignified Python, we strongly prefer LBYL. 65→# WRONG: KeyError as control flow 68→except KeyError: 72→### When Exceptions ARE Acceptable 74→Exceptions are ONLY acceptable at: 76→1. **Error boundaries** (CLI/API level) 80→**Default: Let exceptions bubble up** 82→**When you must use try-except: scope the try block to the single operation that raises.** Don't wrap multiple operations in one try block — each handler should know exactly what failed. Use early returns to keep subsequent code flat. 84→For detailed exception handling patterns including minimal exception scope, B904 chaining, third-party API examples, and anti-patterns, see `references/exception-handling.md`. 118→- `.resolve()` raises `OSError` for non-existent paths 119→- `.is_relative_to()` raises `ValueError` for invalid comparisons 120→- Checking `.exists()` first avoids exceptions entirely (LBYL!) 135→# WRONG: Using exceptions for path validation 140→except (OSError, ValueError): 180→3. **Inline imports only for specific exceptions** (circular deps, TYPE_CHECKING, conditional features) 211→**Acceptable exceptions:** 480→ log.warning("Non-numeric plan_id") 494→- **Raise exception**: When invalid data indicates a programming error or corrupt state 521→- **Exception chaining (B904), minimal exception scope**: `references/exception-handling.md`
17:requires-python = ">=3.11"
session.assistant: Good, Python 3.11+. Now I have all the context needed. Let me re-read the plan carefully to understand exactly what Phase 1 requires:
session.tool_result:
1→# Plan: Convert PR Read Commands to @no_repo_required 2→ 3→**Part of Objective #8832, Node 2.1** 4→ 5→## Context 6→ 7→PR read commands (`pr list`, `pr view`, `pr check`, `pr log`, `pr close`, `pr duplicate-check`) currently require a local git repository because they use `discover_repo_context()` to get `repo_root: Path`, then pass it to `ctx.issues` (GitHubIssues), `ctx.github` (LocalGitHub), and `ctx.plan_store` (PlanBackend) — all of which take `repo_root` as their first parameter. 8→ 9→The objective is to decouple these commands from requiring a local git clone by adding a `--repo owner/repo` flag and routing through RemoteGitHub (REST API-based) when no local repo is available. 10→ 11→**Reference pattern**: The `one-shot` command (`src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py`) was converted in node 1.5 and serves as the canonical example. 12→ 13→## Phase 1: Extend RemoteGitHub with Read Operations 14→ 15→Add read methods to the RemoteGitHub gateway (ABC + Real + Fake). 16→ 17→### Methods to add 18→ 19→| Method | REST Endpoint | Used by | 20→|--------|---------------|---------| 21→| `get_issue(owner, repo, number)` → `IssueInfo \| IssueNotFound` | `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}` | pr view, pr check, pr log, pr close | 22→| `get_issue_comments(owner, repo, number)` → `list[str]` | `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}/comments` | pr check, pr log | 23→| `list_issues(owner, repo, labels, state, limit)` → `list[IssueInfo]` | `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues?labels=...&state=...` | pr list (via PlanListService) | 24→| `get_prs_referencing_issue(owner, repo, number)` → `list[PRReference]` | `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}/timeline` | pr close | 25→| `close_issue(owner, repo, number)` → `None` | `PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/{number}` | pr close | 26→| `close_pr(owner, repo, number)` → `None` | `PATCH /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}` | pr close | 27→| `check_auth_status()` → `tuple[bool, str \| None, str \| None]` | `GET /user` | pr list (creator filter) | 28→ 29→### Files to modify 30→
... [155 lines omitted] ...
60→3. Else → raise `UserFacingCliError` 153→- Verify error handling when neither `--repo` nor local repo available

1→"""Command to dispatch a task for fully autonomous remote execution. 2→ 3→No local planning — the remote Claude explores the codebase, plans the change, 4→implements it, and creates a PR. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk one-shot "fix the import in config.py" 8→ erk one-shot --file prompt.md 9→ erk one-shot "add type hints to utils.py" --model opus 10→ erk one-shot "fix the typo in README.md" --dry-run 11→ erk one-shot "rename issue_number to plan_number in impl_init.py" --plan-only 12→ erk one-shot "fix config bug" --repo owner/repo 13→""" 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk.cli.commands.implement_shared import normalize_model_name 20→from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_remote_dispatch import ( 21→ OneShotDispatchParams, 22→ dispatch_one_shot_remote, 23→) 24→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 25→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError 26→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 27→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub 28→from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.real import RealRemoteGitHub 29→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 30→

1→"""CLI error handling utilities with styled output. 2→ 3→This module provides the Ensure class for asserting invariants in CLI commands 4→with consistent, user-friendly error messages. All errors use red "Error:" prefix 5→for visual consistency. 6→ 7→Domain-Specific Methods: 8→- Git state validations (branch checks, worktree existence, clean state) 9→- Configuration validations (required fields, format checks) 10→- Argument validations (count, type, range) 11→- File/path validations (readable, writable, not hidden) 12→- String/collection validations (non-empty, non-null) 13→- External tool validations (gh CLI installed) 14→""" 15→ 16→from __future__ import annotations 17→ 18→import shutil 19→from pathlib import Path 20→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, TypeVar 21→ 22→import click 23→ 24→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.disabled import GraphiteDisabled, GraphiteDisabledError 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→ 27→if TYPE_CHECKING: 28→ from erk.core.context import ErkContext 29→ 30→T = TypeVar("T")

session.assistant: The plan mentions `@no_repo_required` but it doesn't actually exist in the codebase. Looking at the one-shot command, it doesn't use a `@no_repo_required` decorator either - it simply checks `isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)`. The plan title mentions it but the actual pattern is just `isinstance` checks plus `--repo` option. Let me now understand how `ctx.repo` gets set as `NoRepoSentinel`:
session.tool_result:
35:from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel as NoRepoSentinel 36-from erk_shared.context.types import RepoContext as RepoContext 37- 38-# Import ABCs and fakes from erk_shared.core -- 193: repo=NoRepoSentinel(), 194- repo_info=None, 195- http_client=FakeHttpClient(), 196- dry_run=dry_run, -- 226: repo: RepoContext | NoRepoSentinel | None = None, 227- repo_info: RepoInfo | None = None, 228- package_info: ErkPackageInfo | None = None, 229- remote_github: RemoteGitHub | None = None, -- 259: repo: Optional RepoContext or NoRepoSentinel. If None, uses NoRepoSentinel(). 260- repo_info: Optional RepoInfo. If None, stays None. 261- dry_run: Whether to enable dry-run mode (default False). 262- debug: Whether to enable debug mode (default False). -- 401: repo = NoRepoSentinel() 402- 403- # Apply dry-run wrappers if needed (matching production behavior) 404- # Note: DryRunLocalGitHub composes DryRunGitHubIssues internally for github.issues -- 588: if not isinstance(repo, NoRepoSentinel): 589- try: 590- remote_url = git.remote.get_remote_url(repo.root, "origin") 591- owner, name = parse_git_remote_url(remote_url) --
